name: Supabase MCP Server (Self-Hosted)
what_it_does: Community MCP server for managing self-hosted Supabase instances end-to-end — covering database operations, authentication, storage, realtime subscriptions, and database migrations. Variant of Supabase MCP targeting teams running Supabase on their own infrastructure rather than Supabase Cloud.
use_cases: Managing self-hosted Supabase database schema and data from backend development agents
Querying and mutating Supabase PostgreSQL databases from application agents
Managing Supabase Auth users and permissions from admin agents
Handling Supabase Storage buckets and files from content management agents
not_for: Supabase Cloud users (use the official Supabase MCP server instead)
best_when: A team running Supabase on self-hosted infrastructure (Docker, Kubernetes) wants AI agents to manage their Supabase instance — database, auth, storage — without going through Supabase Cloud.
avoid_when: You use Supabase Cloud — the official Supabase MCP has better support for cloud features. This variant targets self-hosted deployments specifically.

auth.notes: Supabase service role key and self-hosted instance URL required. Service role key has full database access — handle with care.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — self-hosted Supabase is free (infrastructure costs apply)
pricing.paid_tiers: None for software; your infrastructure costs apply
pricing.notes: Supabase self-hosted is free open source. MCP server is free open source. Only infrastructure hosting costs apply.

performance.rate_limits: Limited by your self-hosted instance capacity

known_agent_gotchas: Service role key has full database access — treat it like a root credential; never expose to untrusted agents
Community server (MisterSandFR) — may lag official Supabase API; verify compatibility with your Supabase version
Self-hosted Supabase version compatibility matters — test against your specific deployment
Database migrations via agents are irreversible — require human confirmation before running migrations
agent_readiness.security_notes: Service role key = full DB access. Self-hosted instance — security depends on your deployment. Use row-level security (RLS) policies. Never expose service role key in client-side contexts.

security_score_components.security_notes: Full DB access via service role key. Implement RLS. Community server — audit before production. Self-hosted security is your responsibility.
